title: hibiscus indicus
intro: Hibiscus indicus, also known as the Indian hibiscus or rose mallow, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family that is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of hibiscus indicus include lowering blood pressure, reducing cholesterol levels, and alleviating symptoms of menopause, as well as its potential anti-cancer properties. Hibiscus indicus can be consumed as a tea, capsule, or tincture, with herbal preparations often involving the use of the plant's flowers, leaves, or roots.
